title: Constraints Analysis — NAICS 221122 Electric Power Distribution

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
9 product constraints from Step 01 enriched and assessed against this VN's host market (221122 DSO). 0 knockouts → **market SURVIVES**. 6 mitigable (cost penalties), 3 none (irrelevant for DSO context).

---

sections.title: Step 07 — Enriched Constraints
sections.content:
| # | Name | Type | Threshold | is_absolute | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | operational | DSO-allocated feeder slot | false | Hosting-capacity on target feeder; upgrade needed where saturated |
| C2 | Land Footprint & Siting | physical | 40–80 m²/MWh | false | Container footprint + fire clearances + Feuerwehr access + Baugenehmigung |
| C3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | regulatory | per VdS 3103 / local Feuerwehr | false | Separation to buildings, hydrants, fire walls for >3 MWh clusters |
| C4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | physical | inherent chemistry | true | Cannot eliminate; only contain via NOVEC 1230, compartmentalization, deflagration vents |
| C5 | Grid-Code Certification Lead Time | regulatory | 6–12 months | false | FGW TR8 Type B plant cert + DSO acceptance on critical path |
| C6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | economic | CN-LFP concentration | false | FX/tariff/CRMA/EU Battery Reg exposure |
| C7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | environmental | −20 to +50 °C | false | HVAC keeps internal setpoint; extremes cost efficiency and cycle life |
| C8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | physical | 10–15 % AC-AC | true | Thermodynamic floor: PCS + internal resistance + auxiliary HVAC |
| C9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | economic | €250–400 / kWh installed | false | Below floor, arbitrage/peak-shave case closes; above, payback >7 yr |

**Type coverage:** physical (3), chemical (0 — folded into C4 thermal-runaway chemistry), operational (1), economic (2), regulatory (2), environmental (1). Chemical left empty: process media is sealed inside cells; no external chemical compatibility requirement exists for grid-side BESS.

**Affected markets hint (from Step 01):** DSO, IPP, C&I landlord, data-center, HPC, cold storage, wind/solar parks.

---

sections.title: Step 08 — Compatibility vs NAICS 221122 (DSO-owned Netzbooster)
sections.content:
### Market context (from 05_market_discovery + VN file)

- **Customer persona:** regulated DSO (Bayernwerk, Netze BW, Stromnetz Hamburg, Westnetz)
- **Typical asset:** 10–250 MW Netzbooster for feeder relief, voltage support, substation-upgrade deferral
- **Regulatory regime:** EnWG + §13/§14a, VDE-AR-N 4110 (MV) / 4120 (HV), IEC 61850, BSI KRITIS
- **Procurement:** EU-tender rules, regulated RAB recovery (BNetzA revenue cap)
- **Reference case:** Fluence 250 MW Kupferzell Grid Booster (TransnetBW); EDF 2025 DSO grid-booster tender

### Per-constraint verdicts

| # | Constraint | Verdict | Cost | Time | Rationale (1 line) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 | MV Grid Connection Capacity | **none** | — | — | DSO IS the grid operator — they own feeder allocation and site Netzboosters precisely to relieve congestion; no external connection application |
| C2 | Land Footprint & Siting | **mitigable** | low | 2–4 mo | DSO land portfolio (existing substation plots, switching-station parcels) available; municipal Baugenehmigung standard process |
| C3 | Fire-Protection Clearance | **mitigable** | low | 2–4 mo | VdS 3103 + local Feuerwehr compliance is standard DSO process; separation walls + hydrant provisioning priced into civil scope |
| C4 | Li-Ion Thermal Runaway Hazard | **mitigable** | medium | 3–6 mo | NOVEC 1230 + deflagration vents + compartmentalization priced into container; IEC 62933-5-2 compliance dossier required |
| C5 | Grid-Code Certification Lead Time | **mitigable** | low | 6–12 mo | DSOs operate this process daily; FGW TR8 Type B is well-trodden, parallelizable with procurement |
| C6 | Battery Supply Chain Concentration | **mitigable** | medium | 3–6 mo | CRMA + EU Battery Reg due-diligence dossier; qualify EU-assembled or non-CN cells for utility procurement preference |
| C7 | Ambient Temperature Envelope | **none** | — | — | German DSO service territory sits entirely within −20 to +50 °C envelope; HVAC handles any excursion |
| C8 | Round-Trip Energy Losses | **none** | — | — | Netzbooster revenue is capacity-based (congestion relief, voltage support, substation deferral), not energy-arbitrage; 10–15 % loss does not invalidate the case |
| C9 | CAPEX Viability Threshold | **mitigable** | high | 12–18 mo | DSOs fund via regulated RAB (BNetzA revenue cap); CAPEX enters rate base. BNetzA §11 EnWG recognition of Netzbooster-as-alternative-to-reinforcement required |

### Aggregate

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Constraints assessed | 9 |
| Knockouts | **0** |
| Mitigable | 6 |
| None | 3 |
| Market status | **SURVIVING** |

### Conservative review (08b) — false-knockout scan

No knockouts to downgrade. Cross-check against common patterns:
- Cost as knockout — none applied (C6, C9 correctly mitigable)
- Regulation as knockout — none applied (C3, C5 correctly mitigable, certification paths exist)
- Temperature as knockout — none applied (C7 none, DE climate within envelope)
- Material incompatibility as knockout — N/A (no chemical constraint present)

**Result:** 0 downgrades, verdicts stand.

---

sections.title: Strategic implication for this VN
sections.content:
- **No physics or regulatory wall** blocks Mein Solar from the DSO Netzbooster L5 segment.
- **Dominant cost penalties** are CAPEX recovery (C9 — requires BNetzA §11 EnWG recognition) and CN-LFP supply-chain due diligence (C6 — utility procurement preference for non-CN cells).
- **Scale gap not captured at constraint level:** 221121 TSO tier (100–500 MW) exceeds Mein Solar's 4.5 MW reference by ≥10×, but within 221122 the typical DSO Netzbooster (10–50 MW) is reachable with the current reference.
- **Competitive barrier is incumbents, not constraints:** Fluence, Siemens Grid Integration, Hitachi Energy, Nidec ASI dominate DSO tenders (Part C of VN file). Constraints do not differentiate — execution and references do.

---

sections.title: Neo4j write preview
sections.content:
```
(Constraint {name, run_id}) — 9 nodes
(NeutralProductGroup {name:"Large-Scale Battery Energy Storage System EPC"})
   -[:has_constraint]-> (Constraint) x9
(Constraint) -[:incompatible_with {compatibility, rationale, mitigation_*}]-> (Market {naics_code:"221122"})
   — 6 edges (mitigable only); 3 none verdicts NOT stored
(Market {naics_code:"221122"})
   — status unchanged (SURVIVING); markets_eliminated does not include 221122
```

---
